content.claim: Implementation of Poland's gymnasium phase-out produced a double-cohort crunch in 2017, with both gymnasium graduates and primary school graduates competing for high school places; in Warsaw alone, 3,173 students did not secure high school placement, leading to unplanned technical school enrollment
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Specific to one city (Warsaw) transition; may not reflect system-wide capacity distribution
Does not disaggregate impact by special educational needs status
Does not establish whether capacity constraints were temporary or structural
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
